attributes.class: login-class ng-untouched ng-pristine ng-valid ion-untouched ion-pristine ion-valid sc-ion-input-md-h sc-ion-input-md-s md input-label-placement-start in-item hydrated
ion-button-style md button button-round button-solid ion-activatable ion-focusable hydrated

reviewSummary.summary: The draft captures the core login form controls but includes duplicate password elements, a decorative background container, brittle locators, inconsistent naming, and is missing important workflow validation and navigation elements required for reliable downstream automation.

issues.message: Two separate elements represent the same Password field control.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the stable parent ion-input element with locator id=password and remove the duplicate native input element using id=ion-input-1.

issues.message: Locator id=ion-input-0 appears framework-generated and brittle across sessions or renders.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a semantic locator such as xpath=//input[@placeholder='User Name'] or a stable application-owned attribute if available.

issues.message: Element name is too generic and inconsistent with other semantic control names.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to password_textbox for consistency and downstream keyword clarity.

issues.message: Background content container is decorative layout structure and not meaningful for automation workflows.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the approved page model.

issues.message: Locator relies primarily on visible text and may be unstable if UI wording changes.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer a locator using stable attributes combined with semantic text fallback, such as xpath=//ion-button[contains(normalize-space(.),'SIGN IN') or @text='Login'].

issues.message: Back button required by the workflow and validation expectations is missing from the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantic Back button element using the stable application identifier id=auto_back_btn.

issues.message: Home button required for unauthenticated navigation validation is missing from the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantic Home button element using the stable application identifier id=auto_home_btn.

issues.message: The generic Failed authentication message required for all negative login validations is missing from the approved page elements.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable authentication_failed_message element using a semantic locator targeting visible Failed text.

issues.message: No explicit page-state or form-state identifier exists to verify the Login page is loaded after navigation.
issues.suggested_fix: Use the Username field or Login button as canonical page-ready indicators in downstream resources.

issues.message: The retained password field should represent the interactive ion-input host rather than the nested native input for more stable automation behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Classify the ion-input element with id=password as the canonical password_textbox element.

approved_elements.name: username_textbox
approved_elements.description: Username input field used for authentication.

approved_elements.name: password_textbox
approved_elements.description: Password input field with masked input behavior.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Returns the user to the Home page without authentication.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Navigates the user to the Home page without authentication.

approved_elements.name: authentication_failed_message
approved_elements.description: Generic authentication failure message displayed for rejected login attempts.
